common.authRequired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.
common.forbidden: You do not have permission to perform this action.
common.serviceUnavailable: Service temporarily unavailable. Please try again.

hero.title: Your Passwords.
hero.subtitle: Public core. Client-side encrypted vault payloads. Documented boundaries.
hero.description: Singra Vault protects your vault data locally on your device before it is synchronized. App-password login uses OPAQUE; OAuth remains a separate sign-in path.

security.title: Highest Security Standards
security.subtitle: We combine strong cryptography with local integrity checks, passkey unlock, and clearly documented limits.

zeroKnowledge.title: Client-side encrypted vault payloads
zeroKnowledge.description: Vault contents are encrypted and decrypted locally. Technical metadata and recovery/sharing paths have separate documented boundaries.

e2ee.title: End-to-End Encryption
e2ee.description: AES-256-GCM encryption with Argon2id (128 MiB) key derivation - stronger than industry standard.

openSource.title: Public core source
openSource.description: The public core is available for review. Premium extensions are delivered separately.

clientSide.title: Client-Side Encryption
clientSide.description: All data is encrypted BEFORE being sent to the server.

postQuantum.title: Post-Quantum Protection for Sharing Keys
postQuantum.description: Security Standard v1 uses ML-KEM-768 + RSA-4096 to wrap sharing and emergency-access keys. Vault contents remain encrypted with AES-256-GCM.

passkeyUnlock.title: Passkey Vault Unlock
passkeyUnlock.description: Unlock your vault with biometrics or a security key using the WebAuthn PRF extension - no password needed.

duressPassword.title: Panic Password
duressPassword.description: A secondary password opens a decoy vault with fake data - protection when forced to reveal your password.

vaultIntegrity.title: Vault Integrity Verification
vaultIntegrity.description: Local digest baselines and trusted snapshots isolate tampered items. Recovery is only possible from an earlier trusted local copy.

passwordManager.title: Password Manager
passwordManager.description: Store all your passwords securely in one place.

generator.title: Password Generator
generator.description: Create strong, unique passwords with one click.

categories.title: Categories & Tags
categories.description: Organize your entries with folders and labels.

crossPlatform.title: Cross-Platform
crossPlatform.description: Works on all devices - desktop, tablet, and mobile.

openSource.title: Transparent & Open Source
openSource.subtitle: Trust through transparency
openSource.description: The public Singra Vault core is available for review. Premium extensions stay separate and are not committed to this repository.

comparison.title: Why Singra Vault?
comparison.subtitle: A fact-based comparison with leading password managers. All data comes from official sources and was reviewed on April 23, 2026.
comparison.disclaimer: All information is based on official public documentation from the respective vendors. Pricing, feature scope, and browser support may change at any time.

pwa.title: How Does the PWA Work?
pwa.subtitle: Singra Vault is a Progressive Web App – a modern web application that works like a native app and is also available offline.

whatIsPwa.title: What is a PWA?
whatIsPwa.description: A Progressive Web App combines the best of web and native apps. You can install it directly from your browser – no app store needed – and it behaves like a real app on your device.

offlineAccess.title: Offline Access
offlineAccess.description: Once you've logged in online, you can access your stored passwords even without an internet connection. Your data is cached locally in an encrypted format.

installable.title: Installable
installable.description: Install Singra Vault on your home screen for quick access. In Chrome/Edge: Menu → 'Install app'. On iOS: Share → 'Add to Home Screen'.

autoSync.title: Automatic Sync
autoSync.description: Changes you make offline are automatically synchronized when you're back online. No data is lost.

localEncryption.title: Local Encryption Stays Active
localEncryption.description: Even offline, all your data is stored encrypted with AES-256-GCM. Your master password is still required to unlock the vault.

offlineCache.title: Encrypted Offline Cache
offlineCache.description: The local cache in IndexedDB contains only encrypted data. Without your master password, the data is unreadable – even if someone gains access to your device.

firstLogin.title: First Login Requires Internet
firstLogin.content: An internet connection is required for the first login and setting up your master password. After that, you can access your vault offline.

masterPassword.title: Master Password Always Required
masterPassword.content: Even in offline mode, you must enter your master password. This is an important security feature – without the password, your data remains encrypted.

offlineChanges.title: Offline Changes Are Saved
offlineChanges.content: When you create new entries or edit existing ones offline, they are saved locally and automatically synchronized when you're back online.

browserSupport.title: Browser Compatibility
browserSupport.content: PWA features are supported by all modern browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari). For the best experience, we recommend Chrome or Edge on desktop and Safari on iOS.

footer.copyright: © {{year}} Singra Vault. Source Available under BSL 1.1 License.

securityWhitepaper.title: Security Whitepaper
securityWhitepaper.subtitle: A factual description of the security architecture – derived directly from the codebase and DB policies.
securityWhitepaper.disclaimer: Note: This page describes the implementation (code), not an external audit. Security boundaries and claims without evidence are documented explicitly.

quickFacts.title: Quick facts
quickFacts.subtitle: What to know at a glance
items: App-owned password login runs only through OPAQUE; there is no legacy password fallback.
Vault item contents are encrypted client-side before storage/transmission.
Key derivation: Argon2id with KDF versions v1 (64 MiB) and v2 (128 MiB).
AES-GCM CryptoKeys are non-extractable; raw bytes are best-effort zeroed after import.
RLS is enabled on relevant tables; policies restrict access to auth.uid().

zeroKnowledge.title: Zero-knowledge (vault items)
zeroKnowledge.summary: Sensitive vault content is stored server-side as ciphertext; decryption happens in the client.
bullets: Vault items are stored as encrypted_data; title/URL metadata is intentionally not stored in plaintext (placeholder + null).
Create/import encrypts before writing to the database; the server never receives vault-item plaintext.
Note: Some structural metadata (e.g., category/tag/collection names) remains plaintext DB fields.

authentication.title: Authentication & OPAQUE
authentication.summary: OAuth/social login is a separate path; app-owned password login uses OPAQUE only.
bullets: For app-owned password login, the password is processed locally by OPAQUE. Edge Functions receive only OPAQUE protocol messages, never a password, password hash, or password-equivalent value.
OPAQUE failures abort login. auth-session blocks direct password POSTs server-side with LEGACY_PASSWORD_LOGIN_DISABLED.

Signup, password reset, and password change create OPAQUE registration records client-side; GoTrue password verifiers are removed and blocked at the database layer so Supabase password grants cannot bypass OPAQUE.

Password reset and password change share the same security logic: email code, additional TOTP or recovery code when account 2FA is active, then OPAQUE re-registration. OAuth/social login does not use this path.
The OPAQUE server static public key is pinned in the client via VITE_OPAQUE_SERVER_STATIC_PUBLIC_KEY. If the pin is missing or mismatched, no app session is created.
The Supabase app session is created only after successful OPAQUE finish and is additionally bound to the OPAQUE sessionKey with an HMAC over access token and user ID.

deviceKeys.title: Device keys & local secret stores
deviceKeys.summary: Device keys strengthen local device binding, but the strength differs by platform.
bullets: Tauri/Desktop stores local secrets through Rust commands in the OS secret store. The Rust layer accepts only narrow key domains such as device-key:<uuid> and vault-integrity:<uuid>; arbitrary renderer key names are rejected.
Web/PWA uses IndexedDB and non-extractable WebCrypto keys as defense in depth. This is not an OS-keychain boundary: compromised same-origin JavaScript can still invoke allowed app operations.
Device-key transfer no longer uses a short PIN. Transfer blobs are sv-dk-transfer-v2 envelopes with per-transfer salt, Argon2id derivation, AES-GCM, and a transfer secret of at least 20 characters.
Legacy/raw transfer blobs are not imported. This is an intentional compatibility break because short PIN-wrapped artifacts were offline brute-forceable.
Local secrets are never treated as a replacement for unlocked vault-key material. A compromised unlocked client remains a full compromise of the active session.

sessionsWebAuthn.title: Sessions, cookies & WebAuthn
sessionsWebAuthn.summary: Sessions limit account access; WebAuthn is bound to concrete challenge, origin, and RP contexts.
bullets: The BFF session uses an HttpOnly cookie for refresh material. The former JS-readable sessionStorage fallback is cleared during hydration and is no longer written.
The default cookie lifetime is reduced to 14 days. Access tokens remain short-lived; account access is still a sensitive bearer-token risk and is not the same as vault unlock.
WebAuthn registration and authentication return a challengeId. Verification loads exactly that challenge by user_id, type, and ID; parallel 'latest challenge' flows are no longer accepted.
The expected RP ID and origin come from the stored challenge scope. Web, PWA, and Tauri are not treated as automatically sharing one passkey surface.
Passkey PRF can enable vault unlock only when credential assertion, PRF output, and the server-stored wrappedMasterKey material all match.

recoveryEmergency.title: Recovery & Emergency Access
recoveryEmergency.summary: Recovery and Emergency Access are alternative key-access paths and therefore explicit parts of the trusted computing base.
bullets: Account recovery does not restore plaintext passwords. Email code, optional account 2FA, and OPAQUE re-enrollment create new login data; vault-key material must then be made usable separately.
Vault reset is a destructive recovery operation guarded by a short-lived one-time server challenge. It does not restore old vault contents.
Emergency Access stores the grantor vault key hybrid-wrapped for the trustee. Current paths require ML-KEM-768 + RSA-OAEP and block new legacy RSA-only encrypted_master_key writes.
Trustees receive access only after invite acceptance, setup, request, and wait period. RLS exposes grantor vault ciphertext only when status=granted, granted_at is set, and PQ key material exists.
The feature remains documented as an alternative access path: a compromised trustee account plus available trustee private keys can open the grantor vault after a successful Emergency Access flow.

Hybrid/PQ key wrapping strengthens the trustee key path against future cryptographic attacks, but it does not solve trust-boundary issues. It does not protect against compromised trustee accounts, trustee devices, or unlocked clients.

cryptography.title: Cryptography & KDF
cryptography.summary: AES-256-GCM for data; Argon2id for key derivation; KDF is migrated to stronger parameters when needed.
bullets: AES-GCM: 96-bit IV, 128-bit auth tag; output format is base64(IV || ciphertext || tag).
Fixed KDF versions: v1 = 64 MiB, v2 = 128 MiB (Argon2id).
KDF auto-migration after successful unlock: upgrade may be skipped on OOM to stay device-compatible.

memory.title: Keys & memory clearing
memory.summary: Best-effort: raw key bytes are zeroed; lock removes key references. JavaScript has limits.
bullets: Raw bytes derived from Argon2id are overwritten via Uint8Array.fill(0) after CryptoKey import (best-effort).
Vault lock removes references to encryptionKey/integrityKey and clears session indicators.
SecureBuffer exists as defense-in-depth (explicit destroy() + FinalizationRegistry fallback), but JS/GC cannot guarantee memory wiping.
Strings (e.g., decrypted passwords) cannot be wiped in-place in JS; we can only drop references and rely on GC.

clipboard.title: Clipboard protection
clipboard.summary: Copied secrets are auto-cleared after 30 seconds – only if the user hasn't overwritten them.
bullets: writeClipboard() schedules auto-clear after 30s.
Only clears if clipboard still equals last written value (won't clobber user content).
Limitation: clipboard APIs can be restricted by browser permissions/focus.

xssSameOrigin.title: XSS and same-origin model
xssSameOrigin.summary: Client-side encryption protects stored ciphertexts, but a compromised same-origin app context is not a hard secret boundary.
bullets: Untrusted vault data such as titles, usernames, URLs, notes, categories, TOTP fields, search results, error messages, and filenames is rendered as text. There is no production Markdown/HTML renderer for vault contents.

Same-origin JavaScript from stored XSS, reflected XSS, DOM-XSS, supply-chain compromise, or compromised browser extensions can misuse normal app functions in an unlocked context. It can read an unlocked vault, invoke crypto operations, query IndexedDB/offline data, manipulate sync, and trigger clipboard, export, and download flows.
Non-extractable WebCrypto CryptoKeys reduce key-export risk, but they do not stop compromised app JavaScript from invoking allowed encrypt/decrypt operations.
URL navigation is limited to allowed schemes and internal redirects. javascript:, data:, and open redirects are not accepted as navigation targets.

File attachments are not rendered inline as HTML, SVG, XML, or PDF. Plaintext files are written only as local downloads after decryption; download filenames are normalized and stripped of control characters, path separators, bidi controls, and reserved Windows names.

Production CSP, safe-sink rules, and React text rendering are defense in depth. Trusted Types has been tested but is deferred until runtime compatibility is safe. These measures do not replace correct output encoding or dependency/build-chain integrity.

rls.title: Database access (Supabase RLS)
rls.summary: Row Level Security is enabled; policies allow only own rows or explicit memberships.
bullets: Owner model: many tables use auth.uid() = user_id (e.g., vault_items, file_attachments, categories, tags).
Emergency access: trustees get SELECT on vault_items only when status='granted' and grantor_id matches.
Shared collections: access via owner_id or membership in shared_collection_members; policy is implemented without recursion.

attachments.title: File attachments
attachments.summary: File contents, names, types, and metadata are end-to-end encrypted client-side before upload.
bullets: Every file gets a random file key; every chunk is encrypted with AES-256-GCM, a unique nonce, and AAD.
The encrypted manifest contains file name, extension, MIME type, original size, chunk list, hashes, and preview information. Plaintext columns remain opaque placeholders only.
Storage paths contain no filenames or extensions. Technical metadata remains visible: owner binding, upload time, number/size of ciphertext chunks, and access patterns.
Downloads stream decrypted chunks through File System Access or Tauri file I/O when available; older browsers use a documented Blob fallback.
Rollback is detected through versioned manifests, a manifest root, chunk AAD, and local last-seen checkpoints when a trusted previous state exists.

sharing.title: Sharing (collections & emergency access)
sharing.summary: Sharing is based on DB membership/roles plus asymmetric cryptography for key wrapping.
bullets: Shared collections use owner/member + permission (view/edit) in shared_collection_members.
Emergency access controls trustee access via status and grantor/trustee linkage.
Sharing/emergency keys are wrapped client-side with asymmetric cryptography; current paths use ML-KEM-768 + RSA-OAEP 4096 for key wrapping.

offline.title: Offline mode
offline.summary: Offline snapshots + mutation queue are kept in IndexedDB and checked against a server-side sync head.
bullets: OfflineVaultSnapshot stores per-user snapshots (items, categories) and queued mutations.
Offline unlock stores salt + verifier in snapshot (no plaintext master password).
Each known remote snapshot also stores the last accepted server-side vault revision. A lower later server state is blocked as a rollback/stale snapshot and must not overwrite the local cache.

Offline mutations remember their base revision and are synchronized through apply_vault_mutation() as compare-and-swap. If the server has advanced, the mutation remains queued for conflict-aware handling.
Limitation: browser storage can be wiped; a new device without a local high-water mark cannot cryptographically identify an old but internally consistent server state as a rollback by itself.

categories.title: Category system & delete behavior
categories.summary: Category name, color, and icon are stored encrypted; items also carry their category inside the encrypted item payload.
bullets: When an item is created or edited, the category assignment is stored in encrypted_data as categoryId. The old plaintext category_id column is read only as a legacy/migration fallback and then cleaned up.
When a category is deleted, loadItemsInCategory() first finds affected items by decrypting each local item payload. That is how the client knows which items belong to that category.
The dialog exposes two paths: deleting only the category re-encrypts affected items with categoryId=null; deleting category and items also removes those item rows.
applyOfflineCategoryDeletion() writes category removal and item changes in one local snapshot update. That prevents the UI from alternating between old and new category state.
Category icons come from a fixed emoji allowlist. Free SVG input is blocked so category icons cannot become an XSS or HTML injection path.

integrity.title: Tamper detection & quarantine
integrity.summary: The client stores an encrypted local integrity baseline and a trusted snapshot. Suspicious items are isolated, while category drift blocks normal access.
bullets: The current vault state is checked locally as a canonical snapshot over encrypted item and category data; the server never receives plaintext for this check.
Item drift leads to quarantine instead of silent rendering: changed ciphertext, missing items, and unknown server items are surfaced separately.
The baseline stores separate digests for items and categories. Item drift can be quarantined; category drift blocks normal access because categories affect vault structure and visibility.
Legitimate local changes call refreshIntegrityBaseline() with the affected item/category IDs. Only those IDs may update the baseline; arbitrary database changes are not trusted.
The local integrity check works with the sync head: a server state below the last locally accepted revision is not accepted as a new healthy baseline.
Recovery is only possible from an already trusted local copy. Without such a copy there is no cryptographic recovery path.
Tampered categories or unreadable local baselines block normal access. In that case only Safe Mode, local export, or a vault reset remain.

headers.title: Browser hardening (headers/CSP)
headers.summary: Production and development CSP are separated; production is much tighter than Vite dev/HMR.
bullets: Production Web/PWA sets CSP through the Vercel header and an additional build-time meta CSP: default-src 'self', script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval', object-src 'none', base-uri 'self', form-action 'self', frame-ancestors 'none'.
Production script-src contains no 'unsafe-inline' and no general 'unsafe-eval'. 'wasm-unsafe-eval' remains required for WASM-based crypto/KDF dependencies.

Production connect-src is limited to the app itself, Supabase project URLs/WebSockets, and the HIBP API. img-src allows 'self', data:, and blob: for local previews; worker-src allows 'self' and blob: for service-worker/worker flows.

Trusted Types was tested in the production browser build, but is not enforced: current service-worker, Helmet, and library paths produce runtime errors under require-trusted-types-for 'script'. The target rule remains: clean up safe sinks first, then reintroduce Trusted Types as report-only or a pilot.
Development CSP remains separate and allows localhost/ws/unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval for Vite/HMR. These development exceptions must not be copied into production deployments.
Tauri has its own production CSP without localhost/ws development sources; devCsp remains separate for local desktop development.

limitations.title: Limits & threat model
limitations.summary: Transparency: what risks remain (as with any web password manager).
bullets: Browser/PWA storage is not a hard secret boundary against compromised same-origin JavaScript. IndexedDB, Cache API, non-extractable CryptoKeys, and app services can be misused through allowed APIs from a compromised app context.
If delivered client code is compromised (supply chain/XSS), it can steal secrets during unlock or read the unlocked vault through normal app functions.
Malware on the device, a compromised browser, or a compromised unlocked Tauri process can read the vault while unlocked.

Metadata trade-offs: IDs, timestamps, and ownership links remain structural metadata. Core vault metadata such as item title/URL and category name/icon/color is encrypted; other product areas may still need plaintext names.
File rollback protection is detection, not an absolute guarantee: without a local checkpoint or external audit system, a server can serve an old valid ciphertext state to a new device.
File-size padding is not implemented for attachments yet; ciphertext size, chunk count, and access patterns remain visible.

There is no external security-audit evidence. Focused XSS/Emergency/build checks can be evidenced; the complete Premium test run remains a separate follow-up because of existing Duress and React double-identity test-infrastructure issues.

references.title: Further references
references.subtitle: General standards/guides that inform our decisions

links.owaspCsp: OWASP Content Security Policy Cheat Sheet

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: Transparency and security are our top priorities. Learn how we protect your data.

zeroKnowledge.title: Zero-Knowledge-oriented vault encryption
zeroKnowledge.description: Your data belongs only to you.
zeroKnowledge.details:
Vault payloads are encrypted on your device before they are stored or synchronized. Operators should not be able to view decrypted vault contents. Your Master Password is not processed server-side.

Transparency note: The product is not strict Zero-Knowledge in every function. IDs, timestamps, ownership references, authentication state, 2FA verification, browser/desktop runtime boundaries, and recovery, emergency, and sharing paths have their own metadata and trust boundaries. We minimize this metadata and force neutral server-side placeholders for new vault items wherever it is technically safe.

general.title: General
general.content: This privacy policy explains the nature, scope, and purpose of the processing of personal data within our online offering.

collection.title: Data Collection
collection.content:
We collect only what is necessary to provide the service:

- Email address (for authentication and account recovery)
- Encrypted vault data (we cannot read the content)
- Server-visible technical metadata such as IDs, ownership references, timestamps, and neutral vault-item placeholders
- Vault verifier/OPAQUE data for authentication and unlock checks, not your plaintext password
- Server-side encrypted 2FA/TOTP secret (for two-factor authentication)
- Authentication metadata (login time)

Vault Health note: For the analysis feature, passwords are evaluated locally after decryption in your active session (e.g. strength, age, and reuse checks). No additional persistent server-side storage of plaintext passwords is performed.

security.title: Security Measures
security.content:
To protect your data, we use modern encryption technologies:

- AES-256-GCM for vault payload encryption
- Argon2id for key derivation
- Client-side encryption for vault contents and sensitive vault metadata
- Server-side encryption for stored 2FA/TOTP secrets
- HTTPS/TLS for all transmissions

Cryptographic operations for vault payloads happen locally. Browser storage is not an OS-keychain boundary; the stronger local secret path applies to Tauri/Desktop.

storage.title: Data Storage
storage.content: Your encrypted data is securely stored in our cloud database (Supabase). Backups are created regularly and are also encrypted.

cookies.title: Cookies & Local Data
cookies.content: We use local storage technologies (LocalStorage and IndexedDB) to store your settings (e.g., theme, language), session information, and encrypted offline vault cache for PWA use. We do not use third-party tracking cookies.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.content:
You have the right at any time to:

• Information about your stored data
• Correction of incorrect data
• Deletion of your account and all data
• Export of your data in a machine-readable format

banner.title: We respect your privacy
banner.description: We use cookies to ensure you get the best experience on our website. Some are essential, others help us improve the user experience.

settings.title: Cookie Settings
settings.description: Here you can decide which cookies you want to allow.

necessary.title: Necessary
necessary.description: These cookies are essential for the website to function (e.g. login status). They cannot be disabled.

optional.title: Optional (Preferences)
optional.description: Preferences like Theme (Light/Dark), Language, and Auto-Lock Timer. If disabled, these settings will not be saved permanently.

login.title: Welcome back
login.subtitle: Sign in to open your vault.

login.subtitleReturning: Sign in to open your vault.

signup.passwordRequirements: At least 12 characters with uppercase/lowercase, numbers, and special characters

masterPassword.title: Create Master Password
masterPassword.subtitle: This password encrypts all your data. It is never sent to our servers.
masterPassword.warning: If you forget your master password, we cannot recover your data!

unlock.title: Unlock Vault
unlock.subtitle: Enter your master password to access your data
unlock.sessionRestore: Please re-enter your master password to continue your session.

vault.offlineSaved: Saved offline. Will sync automatically when internet is available.

vault.offlineMode: Offline mode active: changes are stored locally.

empty.subtitle: Add your first entry to get started.

search.placeholder: Search by name, URL, or tags...

item.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this entry?

integrity.quarantineDescription: These items were isolated because their trust basis no longer matches. They are not decrypted as normal vault entries.
integrity.quarantineRecoveryDescription: These items remain isolated until you restore them from a trusted local copy, delete them deliberately, or accept the deletion.
integrity.onlyQuarantinedTitle: Only quarantined items are currently available
integrity.onlyQuarantinedDescription: Normal entries are currently unavailable. Review the quarantine notices and resolve the affected items deliberately.
integrity.reasonMissingOnServer: The item is missing from the current vault state.
integrity.reasonUnknownOnServer: An unknown item was found without a trusted baseline.
integrity.reasonUnknown: This item could no longer be confirmed as trustworthy.
integrity.restoreSuccess: The last trusted local version was restored.
integrity.acceptMissingSuccess: The deletion was accepted and the local trust baseline was updated.
integrity.noTrustedLocalCopy: No trusted local copy is available on this device for recovery.
integrity.confirmDeleteDescription: The suspicious item will be removed from the current vault state. This action cannot be undone.
integrity.confirmAcceptMissingDescription: The missing item will be removed from the trusted local baseline. Recovery will no longer be possible unless another local copy exists.
integrity.safeModeDescription: Safe Mode works exclusively with the last trusted local snapshot. Changes are disabled.
integrity.safeModeNotice: Safe Mode is read-only. Review and export your items if needed before resetting the vault.
integrity.revalidationHint: Check the vault again when the connection is stable.
integrity.resetChallengeExpired: The vault reset confirmation expired. Please try again.
integrity.resetFailed: The vault could not be reset.

settings.title: Settings
settings.footer: Your data is encrypted locally and stored securely.

vaultLockedNotice.title: Vault is currently locked
vaultLockedNotice.description: Account and billing settings remain available. Vault-related settings require unlocking your vault.

desktopLegal.title: Legal & information
desktopLegal.description: Privacy, imprint, and security documentation remain available on every platform.

account.title: Account
account.description: Manage your account and login credentials.
account.deleteConfirmDesc: This action cannot be undone. All your data, passwords, and settings will be permanently deleted.
account.deleteSuccessDesc: Your account and all data have been deleted.
account.deleteVaultWarningDesc: Export your data before deletion if you want to keep it. Account deletion is irreversible.
account.deleteTotpOnlyHint: Backup codes cannot authorize account deletion.
account.deleteTwoFactorInvalid: The two-factor code is invalid or expired.

password.title: Change Password
password.minLength: Password must be at least 12 characters long.
password.emailCodeSent: If your account uses an app password, a code was sent to your account email.
password.emailCodeVerified: Email code confirmed. You can now set a new account password.
password.codeInvalid: The code is invalid or expired.
password.socialOnlyDescription: This account uses social login. An app-owned password is not configured for this auth path.

accountDataExport.exportDesc: Includes profile data, security metadata, passkey metadata, and subscription details.

security.title: Security
security.description: Control the security settings of your vault.
security.autoLockDesc: The vault will automatically lock after inactivity. Note: Reloading the page always requires re-entering your master password for security reasons.
security.vaultLockedDesc: Your vault has been successfully locked.

twoFactor.title: Two-Factor Authentication
twoFactor.description: Add an extra layer of security to your account using an authenticator app.
twoFactor.disabledDesc: Your account is not protected by two-factor authentication.

setup.step1Desc: Open your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.) and scan this QR code.
setup.step2Desc: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to verify the setup.
setup.step3Desc: Save these backup codes in a safe place. You can use them if you lose access to your authenticator app.

vault.title: Require 2FA for Vault

data.title: Data
data.description: Export or import your vault data.
data.exportDesc: Exports your vault entries as an unencrypted JSON file. Includes passwords, notes, and Authenticator secrets when those entries exist.
data.importDesc: Import entries from a JSON export file.
data.importConfirmDesc: The entries will be added to your existing vault.
data.importFailed: Import failed. Make sure the file is valid.

support.title: Support
support.description: Create support tickets and track response times.
support.slaDisclaimer: Response times are target values (in the usual case), not guaranteed response deadlines.
support.securityHint: Never send your master password, recovery codes, private keys, or vault content via support messages.
support.subject: Subject
support.messagePlaceholder: Describe your issue in detail (steps, errors, expected result).
support.validation: Please enter at least 3 characters for subject and 10 characters for message.
support.rateLimited: Too many requests. Please wait a moment and try again.
support.averageResponse30d: Average first response time (30 days): {{hours}} h

realtime.fallback: Live updates disconnected, fallback refresh active

chat.title: Chat
chat.newReplyBody: You received a new reply on your support ticket.

danger.title: Danger Zone
danger.deleteWarning: This action cannot be undone. All your data will be permanently deleted.

family.title: Family Organization
family.description: Invite and manage up to 6 premium family members.
family.inviteSent: Invitation sent. If this email is not registered yet, they can sign up first and then accept it.

sharedCollections.title: Shared Collections
sharedCollections.description: Create collections for sharing vault items with family members.
sharedCollections.masterPasswordDescription: To create your first shared collection, we need to provision hybrid key material for your account.
sharedCollections.masterPasswordRequired: Master password is required to provision key material.
sharedCollections.rotateKeyWarning: All items will be re-encrypted. This may take some time.

admin.title: Admin Area
admin.description: Internal operations for support, team roles, and permission management.
admin.accessDeniedDescription: You don't have permission to access the internal admin area.
admin.accessDeniedMissingRole: Your account is not part of the internal team (admin/moderator).
admin.accessDeniedMissingPermissions: Your internal team account is missing admin permissions (e.g. {{permissions}}).

support.title: Support Inbox
support.noAccess: You don't have permission to read the internal support inbox.
support.selectTicket: Select a ticket on the left.
support.conversationDescription: Live thread with the requester and internal notes for the support team.
support.workflowDescription: Update status and review the operational ticket details in one place.
support.overviewDescription: Map the requesting account and plan quickly.
support.timelineDescription: Created, updated, and response milestones at a glance.
support.replyPlaceholder: Write a user reply or internal note...

subscriptionAssigner.title: Assign subscription
subscriptionAssigner.description: Manually assign a subscription tier to the current user.
subscriptionAssigner.reasonPlaceholder: Explain why this manual assignment is needed.
subscriptionAssigner.errorMissingUser: Please provide a target user ID.
subscriptionAssigner.errorReasonTooShort: Reason must be at least 3 characters.

team.title: Team & Permissions
team.subscriptionDescription: Select a registered user first or enter a user ID manually below.
team.noAccess: You don't have permission to manage roles or permissions.
team.membersDescription: Manage internal roles for team members.
team.userDirectoryDescription: Browse all registered accounts, copy user IDs, and prefill users directly for team or subscription actions.
team.userDirectorySearchPlaceholder: Search by email, name, user ID, or tier

summary.registeredUsersDescription: All accounts currently available for internal actions.
summary.internalMembersDescription: Accounts currently assigned to the internal team.
summary.paidUsersDescription: Accounts currently on Premium or Families.

team.permissionsDescription: Manage role permissions without code deployment.

deleteUser.loadError: Failed to load the deletion preview.
deleteUser.executeError: Failed to delete the user completely.
deleteUser.success: User and owned data were deleted permanently.
deleteUser.successWithWarnings: User was deleted, but {{count}} cleanup warnings were recorded in the audit log.
deleteUser.stepOneDescription: Review exactly which data will be removed or anonymized before continuing.
deleteUser.stepTwoDescription: This action is permanent and cannot be undone.
deleteUser.authManagedDescription: These Auth tables are cleaned up by the server-side user deletion itself.
deleteUser.storageDescription: All linked Storage objects are removed before deleting the Auth user because Supabase blocks user deletion while owned objects still exist.
deleteUser.preservedDescription: These references are anonymized instead of deleted so data owned by other accounts is not damaged.
deleteUser.inventoryEmpty: No entries found in this section.
deleteUser.doubleConfirmNote: Two manual confirmations are required to reduce operator mistakes.

blockingReasons.self_delete_blocked: The currently signed-in admin cannot delete their own account.
blockingReasons.last_admin: The last remaining admin cannot be deleted.
blockingReasons.user_not_found: The target user could not be found.

fileAttachments.title: File Attachments
fileAttachments.dropzone: Drop files here or click to upload
fileAttachments.pendingUploadFailed: {{count}} attachment(s) could not be uploaded. The entry was saved without those attachments.
fileAttachments.pendingUploadRequiresOnline: Attachments were not uploaded because the entry was saved offline. Please add them again after sync.

categories.description: Organize your entries with custom categories.
categories.name: Name
categories.svgDisabled: SVG icons are disabled for security reasons.
categories.deletedOnlyCategory: Category deleted. {{count}} items remain without a category.
categories.deleteConfirmDesc: The category will be deleted. Items in this category won't be deleted, but will no longer have a category.
categories.deleteWithItemsDesc: This category contains {{count}} items. You can delete only the category or delete the items too.

authenticator.title: Authenticator
authenticator.subtitle: Manage your 2FA codes
authenticator.emptyDesc: Add a new account to get started with 2FA.

vaultHealth.title: Vault Health
vaultHealth.subtitle: Security scanning and reports
vaultHealth.analyzed: {{count}} password entries analyzed out of {{total}} total entries.
vaultHealth.allGoodDesc: No critical password issues were found.

emergency.title: Emergency Access
emergency.description: Manage who can access your vault in emergencies.
emergency.unlockRequired: Unlock your vault to manage emergency access settings.
emergency.inviteDesc: Enter the email of the person you want to trust with emergency access.
emergency.inviteSent: Invitation sent. If this email is not registered yet, they can sign up first and then accept it.
emergency.inviteAccepted: Invitation accepted. Key pair generated and stored.
emergency.fetchError: Failed to load emergency access data.
emergency.noGrantors: You are not a trusted contact for anyone.
emergency.waitTimeDetail: How long after a request before access is granted.
emergency.setupError: Failed to configure access. Check your password.
emergency.confirmSetupDesc: Enter your master password to encrypt your vault key for this contact.
emergency.noKey: No master key found. Setup not complete.

subscription.premium_description: Advanced security and features for power users.
subscription.families_description: Secure passwords for the whole family.

support.families_sla: Priority support - usually within 24h (owner + all active family members)
support.sla_note: Support response times are target values in the usual case.

subscription.free_security_note: AES-256-GCM + Argon2id included for all users
subscription.legal_info: All prices include VAT. Subscriptions renew automatically unless canceled before the end of the billing period.
subscription.refund_info: Refunds are handled individually through our support team.
subscription.checkout_description: You will be redirected to Stripe to subscribe to {{plan}}.
subscription.widerruf_info: Notice on Right of Withdrawal: As this involves digital content, we ask for your consent before purchase in accordance with § 356 (5) BGB.
subscription.widerruf_consent_execution: I expressly request that you begin executing the contract before the withdrawal period expires.
subscription.widerruf_consent_loss: I acknowledge that I will lose my right of withdrawal upon full contract execution.
subscription.paymentSuccessfulBody: Your subscription has been updated successfully.
subscription.cancel_description: Are you sure you want to cancel your {{tier}} subscription? You will retain access until {{date}}.
subscription.cancel_effect_access: You keep Premium access until the end of the current period.
subscription.cancel_effect_features: After that, you will automatically switch to the FREE plan.
subscription.cancel_effect_data: Your stored data will be preserved.

reauth.title: Confirm account password
reauth.description: For this sensitive action, confirm your account password again.
reauth.authRequired: Your session is no longer valid. Please sign in again.
reauth.twoFactorRequired: Two-factor verification is required. Please sign in again.
reauth.accountDeleteContext: Please confirm your identity before deleting your account.
reauth.subscriptionCancelContext: Please confirm your identity before canceling your subscription.
reauth.vaultResetContext: Please confirm your identity before irreversibly resetting your vault.
reauth.confirmationHint: For social-login accounts without an account password, confirm this action with the safety keyword.

passkey.title: Passkeys
passkey.description: Use biometrics or a security key to unlock your vault without typing your master password.
passkey.infoText: Passkeys use the PRF extension to derive an encryption key from your authenticator. Your master password remains as a fallback and can always unlock the vault.
passkey.noPlatformAuth: No platform authenticator detected. You can still use a security key (e.g. YubiKey).
passkey.passwordRequired: Master password is required to register a passkey.
passkey.unlockRequired: Unlock your vault to register new passkeys.
passkey.passwordHint: Required to securely link the passkey to your vault encryption key.
passkey.registeredWithPrf: Passkey registered! You can now unlock your vault with this passkey.
passkey.registeredWithoutPrf: Passkey registered for authentication, but this device does not support vault unlock (no PRF). You will still need your master password.
passkey.deleteDescription: This will permanently remove "{{name}}". You will no longer be able to unlock your vault with this passkey. Your master password will still work.
passkey.unlockFailed: Passkey unlock failed. Please try your master password.

postQuantum.title: Post-Quantum Protection for Sharing Keys
postQuantum.description: Security Standard v1 protects sharing and emergency-access keys with ML-KEM-768 + RSA-4096. It does not change AES-256-GCM encryption for your vault items.
postQuantum.infoText: The post-quantum path applies to key wrapping and key exchange for Shared Collections and Emergency Access. Vault payloads remain protected with conventional symmetric AES-256-GCM.
postQuantum.enableDescription: Hybrid key material is provisioned automatically when a sharing or emergency-access key needs to be wrapped.
postQuantum.enableSuccess: Post-quantum protection for sharing keys has been enabled successfully!
postQuantum.premiumRequired: Post-quantum protection for sharing keys requires a Premium or Families subscription.

duress.title: Panic Password
duress.description: Set up a secondary password that opens a decoy vault instead of your real one.
duress.howItWorksDescription: A panic password (also called duress password) is a secondary password that, when entered, shows a fake vault with decoy items instead of your real data. This protects you if you're forced to reveal your password (e.g., at border control or under threat).
duress.warning: Important: Never share your panic password with anyone. If an adversary learns both passwords, this protection is ineffective.
duress.unlockRequired: Unlock your vault to manage panic password settings.
duress.premiumRequired: Panic password requires a Premium or Families subscription.
duress.setupDescription: Create a secondary password that will show a decoy vault. Make sure it's different from your real master password but still easy to remember.
duress.masterPasswordRequired: Master password is required to verify your identity
duress.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
duress.differentPasswordWarning: Your panic password must be different from your master password. Choose something plausible but distinct.
duress.setupSuccessDescription: You can now use your panic password to show a decoy vault.
duress.setupFailed: Failed to set up panic password
duress.disableDescription: This will remove your panic password. Your decoy vault items will become inaccessible.
duress.disableWarning: After disabling, you will no longer be able to access decoy vault items. This action cannot be undone.
duress.disableSuccessDescription: Your panic password has been removed.
duress.changeDescription: Enter your current panic password and choose a new one.
duress.changeSuccessDescription: Your panic password has been updated.

passwordStrength.pwned: This password was found in {{count}} data breaches!
passwordStrength.pwnedWarning: We strongly recommend using a different password.

deviceKey.title: Device Key
deviceKey.description: An additional 256-bit local factor. It helps against server-only compromise, but it does not protect against malware, XSS, or malicious browser extensions on this device.
deviceKey.active: Device Key is active on this device. Unlock requires the master password and this local key.
deviceKey.inactive: No Device Key on this device. Enable protection to add a local factor against server-only compromise.
deviceKey.enableDesc: A 256-bit random key will be generated and stored locally. With the UserKey vault format, the UserKey wrapper is re-encrypted with the combined key.
deviceKey.enableWarning: IMPORTANT: If you lose the Device Key and have no export, your vault becomes permanently unrecoverable. Create an export IMMEDIATELY after enabling!
deviceKey.backupAck: I understand that Singra cannot restore this Device Key. Without this local key or a prior export, this Device-Key-protected vault cannot be decrypted.
deviceKey.webBoundary: Web/PWA storage is browser-local. It helps against server-only compromise, but it does not protect against XSS, malicious extensions, malware, or deleted browser data.
deviceKey.desktopBoundary: Desktop stores the Device Key in the OS keychain. This is stronger than browser storage, but it does not protect against malware or a compromised authorized app renderer.
deviceKey.enableSuccess: Device Key enabled. Create an export now for other devices!
deviceKey.exportDesc: The Device Key is encrypted with a transfer secret. Use the generated random secret or at least 20 random characters.
deviceKey.exportInstructions: Copy this code and enter it together with the transfer secret on the new device. Old short PIN transfer codes are no longer accepted.
deviceKey.transferWarning: Treat the transfer code and transfer secret as sensitive. Anyone with both can import the Device Key.
deviceKey.importDesc: Enter the export code and transfer secret from the other device.
deviceKey.importWarning: Import only Device Keys from your own trusted devices. A wrong key will not overwrite a valid local key, but it will not unlock this vault.
deviceKey.importFailed: Import failed. Check the code and transfer secret.

integrity.title: Vault Integrity
integrity.description: Tamper detection for your vault data
integrity.tamperDescription: Your vault data may have been tampered with. Items may have been added, modified, or deleted without your knowledge. Please review your vault contents carefully.
integrity.passkeyNote: Integrity verification is not available for passkey unlock sessions.
